decks.notes: The main focus of this Mardu Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Amped Raptor and Ocelot Pride, backed up by removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Cabal Therapy. The deck also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to provide additional threats and support. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Hexing Squelcher and Thoughtseize helps disrupt the opponent's game plan, allowing the deck to maintain tempo and pressure. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the aggressive strategy while also providing access to key cards like Karakas and Goblin Bombardment.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through countermagic, removal spells, and card advantage engines like Brainstorm and Teferi, Time Raveler. It utilizes a mix of powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi, along with versatile removal like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually gaining an advantage through card selection and incremental advantage. By efficiently answering threats and generating card advantage, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and eventually take control of the game.

Utilizing artifact creatures and synergy with artifacts to quickly play and overwhelm opponents with a combination of efficient creatures, disruption, and mana acceleration. The deck focuses on utilizing cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Urza's Saga to generate value and tempo advantage, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Force of Will. The deck aims to win through aggressive creature beats and disruption, taking advantage of the powerful artifacts in the deck to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent.

This deck focuses on locking down the opponent's resources through a combination of artifact-based control elements and powerful prison effects. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Trinisphere, and Planar Nexus, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and prevent them from executing their strategy effectively. Additionally, the deck incorporates powerful artifact mana acceleration via cards like Grim Monolith and Candelabra of Tawnos, allowing the player to ramp into game-ending threats like Ugin, the Eye of the Storms and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. Overall, the deck seeks to dominate the game through resource denial and overwhelming late-game power.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and fast mana sources to quickly deploy threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Mishra's Bauble enable card advantage and recursion, while cards like Pithing Needle and Chalice of the Void disrupt the opponent's key cards. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive artifact creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Patchwork Automaton, supported by interaction like Force of Will. Urza's Saga provides additional value by tutoring for powerful artifacts to further press the advantage.

This deck focuses on utilizing Cloudpost/Tron lands to ramp quickly into powerful artifacts and big, game-ending threats like Emrakul and Ulamog. The deck utilizes cards like Planar Nexus and The One Ring for extra value while controlling the board with cards like Trinisphere and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. With the help of Karn, the Great Creator, the deck can also shut down opposing strategies by accessing cards from the sideboard. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with its powerful artifacts and massive creatures while maintaining control of the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing the graveyard with cards like Stitcher's Supplier and Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis to quickly bring out powerful creatures. It also utilizes land synergies with Wight of the Reliquary and Sylvan Safekeeper to help protect and strengthen its board presence. The deck includes disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game and set up for a powerful combo finish. Lastly, it includes ramp and toolbox options with cards like Once Upon a Time and Elvish Reclaimer to search for key pieces and support its game plan.

This combo deck aims to quickly mill the entire library to win the game with Thassa's Oracle. It utilizes fast mana acceleration like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, along with rituals like Dark Ritual and Cabal Ritual to power out its spells. Boggart Tramper, Balustrade Spy, and Undercity Informer are key pieces in the mill combo, while Narcomoeba helps trigger additional draws. Pact of Negation and Duress provide protection and disruption, allowing the deck to go off without interruption. Overall, the deck aims to win the game in one explosive turn.

The main concept of this deck is to control the tempo of the game while applying aggressive pressure on the opponent. By utilizing cards like Barrowgoyf, Orcish Bowmasters, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, while maintaining a steady stream of threats. The deck's mana base also supports this strategy with cards like Wasteland and efficient fetch lands to ensure consistent access to the necessary colors of mana. Overall, the deck seeks to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining a proactive approach to winning the game.

This Eldrazi Aggro deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents with powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupt their game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer. The deck utilizes mana acceleration from Ancient Tomb, Eldrazi Temple, and Lotus Petal to deploy threats ahead of schedule. Wasteland and Wastescape Battlemage provide land destruction to further disrupt opponents, while Glaring Fleshraker and Reality Smasher offer big threats that can end the game quickly. The deck is designed to be aggressive and proactive, aiming to win the game before opponents can stabilize.

The deck revolves around quickly getting out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Archon of Cruelty onto the battlefield by casting Show and Tell or Sneak Attack. By utilizing cards like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb for mana acceleration, the deck can bypass its high mana costs. Control elements like Flusterstorm and Force of Will help protect the combo pieces, while Brainstorm and Ponder ensure consistency in drawing the necessary pieces. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its huge creatures before they can establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures and disruptive spells to quickly control the game tempo and apply pressure on the opponent. By combining powerful creatures like Barrowgoyf and Murktide Regent with cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Fatal Push, the deck aims to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also includes card advantage engines like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Brainstorm to ensure consistent draws and maintain control of the game. The mana base is built to support the aggressive playstyle while also disrupting the opponent with cards like Wasteland.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifact prison cards like Trinisphere and The One Ring to lock down the opponent's resources and control the game. Urza's Workshop, Urza's Tower, and Urza's Mine provide mana acceleration to play powerful cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Ugin, Eye of the Storms, and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. Disruptor Flute and Kozilek's Command provide additional disruption, while cards like Pithing Needle and Voltaic Key offer utility and versatility. The main win condition is establishing a dominant board presence and overwhelming the opponent with powerful artifacts and control elements.

The deck focuses on cheating big powerful creatures into play quickly using Show and Tell and Sneak Attack, often bypassing the high mana cost requirement. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, Griselbrand, or Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also includes ways to protect its combo pieces with cards like Force of Will and Flusterstorm. It aims to quickly assemble the necessary pieces to combo off and win the game before the opponent has a chance to respond effectively.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on controlling the game through disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Daze, while also pressuring the opponent with efficient creatures like Barrowgoyf and Murktide Regent. The deck utilizes a mix of disruption, removal, and card advantage to maintain tempo and keep the opponent on the back foot. By leveraging the power of cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Brainstorm, the deck is able to stay ahead on cards while aggressively attacking the opponent's resources. With a strong mana base and versatile threats, this deck aims to outmaneuver and overwhelm the opponent in the early stages of the game.

The main concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to quickly establish a strong board presence by leveraging powerful Eldrazi creatures like Glaring Fleshraker, Thought-Knot Seer, and Reality Smasher. This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by using cards like Chalice of the Void and Wasteland, while also ramping into its bigger threats with cards like Eldrazi Temple and Eye of Ugin. By combining aggressive creature plays with disruptive elements, this deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent and secure a swift victory in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.
